zone_6b_notes: Start indoors 8–10 weeks before last frost (early February in Farmington) — parsley germinates slowly (2–4 weeks). Soak seeds 24 hours before planting to aid germination. Biennial that bolts to seed in its second year; grow as annual in the vegetable garden. Flat-leaf (Italian) parsley has better flavor than curly; curly holds up better as garnish. Survives light frost and can be harvested into November in Zone 6b. Swallowtail butterfly caterpillars (parsleyworm) will defoliate plants — plant extra and let some go for the butterflies.

planting.best_planting_time: Start indoors early February; transplant late April to May
